responsibilities: Confirm the service mode is revision rather than drafting or editing.
Own the loop charter, gates, taskboard, issue decisions, and closeout.
Protect the governing argument from drift and random patching.
Decide when to reopen a section, trigger another revision pass, or hand off downstream.
Verify that the integrated master draft is substantively stable before closeout.
must_not: Must not let the librarian self-accept any gate.
Must not confuse section rewriting with final editing.
Must not close the loop while major structural blockers remain.
Must not preserve outdated draft logic just because it already exists.

responsibilities: Audit the existing draft set and diagnose what is wrong with the paper.
Define the revised governing argument and findings architecture.
Rebuild findings, discussion, and conclusion using the best current evidence and literature.
Integrate retained and rewritten sections into a single authoritative master draft.
Write unresolved-issues and handoff notes for downstream services.
must_not: Must not treat weak old sections as authoritative by default.
Must not self-approve evidence quality or literature integration.
Must not leave the paper as scattered section files at closeout.
Must not hand off to editing while the argument is still unstable.

responsibilities: Independently review diagnosis, argument clarity, revision map quality, section redevelopment, and final integration.
Spot-check evidence provenance in rebuilt findings.
Test whether the discussion genuinely converses with the literature.
Block closure if the draft remains structurally unstable or conceptually weak.
Confirm the integrated master draft is ready for Citalio and/or final editing.
must_not: Must not write or rewrite the paper sections.
Must not approve vague claims of improvement without draft evidence.
Must not ignore repeated framing, placeholder debt, or unstable section logic.
Must not let a better-looking but still incoherent paper pass closeout.

responsibilities: Clarify the intended paper contribution when multiple plausible directions remain.
Resolve disputes about whether the paper should return to drafting or proceed to editing.
Approve major scope changes if the revised paper diverges from the original brief.
Provide final judgement on trade-offs when multiple revision strategies are defensible.
must_not: Should not be asked to make routine gate decisions already covered by the SOP.
Should not be bypassed when the revision materially changes the paper's intended contribution.
